title: Vishnu Chalisa
intro: Shri Vishnu Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Vishnu
description:
॥ Shri Vishnu Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥

Vishnu suniye vinay sevak ki chitlay.
Keerat kuch varnan karun deejai gyan batay.

॥ Chaupai ॥

Namo Vishnu bhagwan kharari,
Kasht nashavan akhil bihari.
Prabal jagat mein shakti tumhari,
Tribhuvan phail rahi ujiyari.
Sundar roop manohar surat,
Saral swabhav mohani murat.
Tan par pitambar ati sohat,
Baijanti mala man mohat.
Shankh chakra kar gada biraje,
Dekhat daitya asur dal bhaje.
Satya dharm mad lobh na gaaje,
Kaam krodh mad lobh na chhaaje.
Santbhakt sajjan manranjan,
Danuj asur dushtan dal ganjan.
Sukh upjaay kasht sab bhanjan,
Dosh mitay karat jan sajjan.
Paap kaat bhav sindhu utaran,
Kasht nashkar bhakt ubaran.
Karat anek roop prabhu dharan,
Keval aap bhakti ke karan.
Dharani dhenu ban tumhin pukara,
Tab tum roop Ram ka dhara.
Bhaar utaar asur dal mara,
Ravan aadik ko sanhara.
Aap Varah roop banaya,
Haranyaksh ko maar giraya.
Dhar matsya tan sindhu banaya,
Chaudah ratnan ko niklaya.
Amilakh asuran dwand machaya,
Roop mohani aap dikhaya.
Devan ko amrit paan karaya,
Asuran ko chhavi se behlaya.
Kurm roop dhar sindhu majhaya,
Mandrachal giri turat uthaya.
Shankar ka tum phand chudaya,
Bhasmasur ko roop dikhaya.
Vedan ko jab asur dubaya,
Kar prabandh unhein dhudhvaya.
Mohit bankar khalhi nachaya,
Usahi kar se bhasm karaya.
Asur Jalandhar ati baldai,
Shankar se un kinh ladai.
Haar paar Shiv sakal banai,
Kin Sati se chhal khal jai.
Sumiran kin tumhein Shivrani,
Batlai sab vipat kahani.
Tab tum bane munishwar gyani,
Vrinda ki sab surati bhulani.
Dekhat teen danuj shaitani,
Vrinda aay tumhein laptani.
Ho sparsh dharm kshati mani,
Hana asur ur Shiv shaitani.
Tumne Dhruv Prahlad ubare,
Hiranyakush aadik khal maare.
Ganika aur Ajamil taare,
Bahut bhakt bhakt bhav sindhu utare.
Harahu sakal santap hamare,
Kripa karahu Hari sirjan haare.
Dekhun main nij darash tumhare,
Deen bandhu bhaktan hitkaare.
Chahat aapka sevak darshan,
Karahu daya apni Madhusudan.
Jaanun nahi yogya jab poojan,
Hoy yagya stuti anumodan.
Sheeldaya santosh sulakshan,
Vidit nahi vratbodh vilakshan.
Karahu aapka kis vidhi poojan,
Kumati vilok hot dukh bhishan.
Karahu pranam kaun vidhisumiran,
Kaun bhanti main karahu samarpan.
Sur muni karat sada sevkai,
Harshit rahat param gati pai.
Deen dukhin par sada sahai,
Nij jan jaan lev apnai.
Paap dosh santap nashao,
Bhav bandhan se mukt karao.
Sut sampati de sukh upjao,
Nij charnan ka daas banao.
Nigam sada ye vinay sunavai,
Padhai sunai so jan sukh paavai.

॥ Doha ॥

Bhakt hridaya mein vaas karein purn kijiyee kaaj.
Shankh chakra aur gada padma he Vishnu Maharaj.
meaning:
॥ Shri Vishnu Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥

Vishnu suniye vinay sevak ki chitlay.
O Lord Vishnu, listen to the prayer of your servant with an attentive mind.
Keerat kuch varnan karun deejai gyan batay.
I wish to describe a bit of your glory, please grant me the knowledge to do so.

॥ Chaupai ॥

Namo Vishnu bhagwan kharari,
I bow to Lord Vishnu, the enemy of the demon Khara.
Kasht nashavan akhil bihari.
You are the destroyer of pain and the omnipresent one.
Prabal jagat mein shakti tumhari,
Your power is supreme in this world.
Tribhuvan phail rahi ujiyari.
The light of your glory spreads across the three worlds.
Sundar roop manohar surat,
Your form is beautiful and your face is enchanting.
Saral swabhav mohani murat.
You have a simple nature and a captivating image.
Tan par pitambar ati sohat,
The yellow garment on your body looks very beautiful.
Baijanti mala man mohat.
Your Vaijayanti garland captivates the mind.
Shankh chakra kar gada biraje,
The Conch, Chakra, and Mace shine in your hands.
Dekhat daitya asur dal bhaje.
Seeing them, the armies of demons and monsters flee.
Satya dharm mad lobh na gaaje,
Truth and righteousness prevail, while greed does not exist.
Kaam krodh mad lobh na chhaaje.
Lust, anger, pride, and greed do not suit you (they do not affect you).
Santbhakt sajjan manranjan,
You bring joy to the hearts of saints, devotees, and good people.
Danuj asur dushtan dal ganjan.
You are the destroyer of the groups of demons and wicked ones.
Sukh upjaay kasht sab bhanjan,
You create happiness and destroy all miseries.
Dosh mitay karat jan sajjan.
By erasing flaws, you turn people into virtuous beings.
Paap kaat bhav sindhu utaran,
You cut through sins and help us cross the ocean of existence.
Kasht nashkar bhakt ubaran.
You destroy pain and save your devotees.
Karat anek roop prabhu dharan,
O Lord, you take on many forms.
Keval aap bhakti ke karan.
Only for the sake of your devotees' devotion.
Dharani dhenu ban tumhin pukara,
When the Earth called upon you in the form of a cow.
Tab tum roop Ram ka dhara.
Then you took the form of Lord Ram.
Bhaar utaar asur dal mara,
You lifted the burden of the Earth and killed the army of demons.
Ravan aadik ko sanhara.
You destroyed Ravan and others like him.
Aap Varah roop banaya,
You took the Varah (Boar) form.
Haranyaksh ko maar giraya.
And struck down the demon Haranyaksh.
Dhar matsya tan sindhu banaya,
Assuming the Matsya (Fish) body, you entered the ocean.
Chaudah ratnan ko niklaya.
And brought out the fourteen gems.
Amilakh asuran dwand machaya,
The demons created a great conflict.
Roop mohani aap dikhaya.
Then you displayed your Mohini form.
Devan ko amrit paan karaya,
You gave nectar (Amrit) to the gods to drink.
Asuran ko chhavi se behlaya.
And beguiled the demons with your beauty.
Kurm roop dhar sindhu majhaya,
Taking the Kurm (Tortoise) form, you churned the ocean.
Mandrachal giri turat uthaya.
And immediately lifted the Mandrachal mountain.
Shankar ka tum phand chudaya,
You freed Lord Shankar from a trap.
Bhasmasur ko roop dikhaya.
And showed your form to Bhasmasur (to destroy him).
Vedan ko jab asur dubaya,
When the demon submerged the Vedas.
Kar prabandh unhein dhudhvaya.
You made arrangements and had them found.
Mohit bankar khalhi nachaya,
Becoming the enchantress, you made the wicked one dance.
Usahi kar se bhasm karaya.
And by his own hand, you caused him to turn to ashes.
Asur Jalandhar ati baldai,
The demon Jalandhar was very powerful.
Shankar se un kinh ladai.
He had fought with Shankar.
Haar paar Shiv sakal banai,
Shivji defeated him completely.
Kin Sati se chhal khal jai.
And you committed a trick with Sati (Vrinda).
Sumiran kin tumhein Shivrani,
The Queen of Shiva (Parvati/Vrinda) remembered you.
Batlai sab vipat kahani.
And told the story of all her troubles.
Tab tum bane munishwar gyani,
Then you became a wise sage.
Vrinda ki sab surati bhulani.
And made Vrinda forget all her memories.
Dekhat teen danuj shaitani,
Watching the mischief of the three demons.
Vrinda aay tumhein laptani.
Vrinda came and embraced you.
Ho sparsh dharm kshati mani,
The touch was considered a loss of righteousness.
Hana asur ur Shiv shaitani.
Then the demon was killed and Shiva destroyed the evil.
Tumne Dhruv Prahlad ubare,
You saved Dhruv and Prahlad.
Hiranyakush aadik khal maare.
And killed wicked demons like Hiranyakashipu.
Ganika aur Ajamil taare,
You liberated Ganika and Ajamil.
Bahut bhakt bhakt bhav sindhu utare.
You have helped many devotees cross the ocean of existence.
Harahu sakal santap hamare,
Remove all our miseries.
Kripa karahu Hari sirjan haare.
O Hari, Creator of the universe, have mercy.
Dekhun main nij darash tumhare,
I wish to behold your divine vision.
Deen bandhu bhaktan hitkaare.
O Friend of the poor and well-wisher of devotees.
Chahat aapka sevak darshan,
Your servant desires your presence.
Karahu daya apni Madhusudan.
O Madhusudan, shower your mercy upon me.
Jaanun nahi yogya jab poojan,
I do not know how to perform your worship worthily.
Hoy yagya stuti anumodan.
May this prayer and praise be accepted by you.
Sheeldaya santosh sulakshan,
Virtue, compassion, and contentment are your qualities.
Vidit nahi vratbodh vilakshan.
I am not aware of your unique vows and divine knowledge.
Karahu aapka kis vidhi poojan,
How should I worship you?
Kumati vilok hot dukh bhishan.
Seeing my ignorance, I feel deep sorrow.
Karahu pranam kaun vidhisumiran,
In what way should I bow and remember you?
Kaun bhanti main karahu samarpan.
In what manner should I surrender to you?
Sur muni karat sada sevkai,
The gods and sages always serve you.
Harshit rahat param gati pai.
And they remain joyful after attaining the highest state.
Deen dukhin par sada sahai,
You are always the helper of the poor and distressed.
Nij jan jaan lev apnai.
Recognizing me as your own, please accept me.
Paap dosh santap nashao,
Destroy my sins, faults, and miseries.
Bhav bandhan se mukt karao.
Liberate me from the bonds of the world.
Sut sampati de sukh upjao,
Grant me children and prosperity, and bring happiness.
Nij charnan ka daas banao.
Make me a servant at your feet.
Nigam sada ye vinay sunavai,
The scriptures always sing this prayer.
Padhai sunai so jan sukh paavai.
Whoever reads or hears this, attains happiness.

॥ Doha ॥

Bhakt hridaya mein vaas karein purn kijiyee kaaj.
Dwell in the heart of your devotee and fulfill my tasks.
Shankh chakra aur gada padma he Vishnu Maharaj.
O Lord Vishnu, wielder of the Conch, Chakra, Mace, and Lotus.

faq.q1: What is the Vishnu Chalisa and what is its significance?
faq.a1: The Vishnu Chalisa is a sacred devotional hymn consisting of 40 verses dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Preserver of the Universe. In Hinduism, Lord Vishnu is the deity responsible for the maintenance and protection of the cosmos. Reciting this Chalisa is a way to express reverence, seek His divine protection, and invite peace and stability into one's life.
faq.q2: What are the primary benefits of reciting the Vishnu Chalisa?
faq.a2: Devotees believe that reciting the Vishnu Chalisa helps bring harmony and positive energy into their lives. The primary benefits include: Mental Peace: It is known to calm the mind and reduce anxiety. Prosperity: Since Lord Vishnu is the consort of Goddess Lakshmi, His worship is often associated with financial stability and overall prosperity. Obstacle Removal: It is believed to provide the strength and patience needed to overcome life’s challenges. Spiritual Growth: Regular recitation fosters a deeper sense of devotion and helps align one’s life with righteous principles (Dharma).
faq.q3: Is there a "best" day to recite it?
faq.a3: You can recite the Vishnu Chalisa on any day. However, Thursday is considered especially auspicious for the worship of Lord Vishnu. Additionally, Ekadashi (the 11th day of the lunar fortnight) is a day of great spiritual significance for His devotees. That said, consistency is the key; reciting it daily is far more effective than observing specific days occasionally.
faq.q4: Are there strict rules or rituals to follow?
faq.a4: Lord Vishnu is known for His simplicity and kindness. There are no rigid or complicated rules to follow. The most important requirement is a sincere heart. Basic practices include: Cleanliness: Taking a bath or washing your hands and face before starting. Atmosphere: Finding a quiet, clean space to focus. Intention: Maintaining a pure and devotional state of mind. External rituals are secondary to the Bhav (deep feeling and devotion) you hold in your heart.
faq.q5: Do I need a Guru or formal initiation to recite it?
faq.a5: No, you do not need a Guru or any formal initiation to recite the Vishnu Chalisa. Lord Vishnu is the universal father figure, and every individual has the right to pray to Him directly. If your intent is pure, your prayers will be heard.
faq.q6: Is it necessary to understand the literal meaning of the verses?
faq.a6: While it is not mandatory, understanding the meaning can significantly deepen your connection to the prayer. When you know what you are reciting, it becomes a conversation with the Divine rather than just a mechanical recitation. Many devotees find that reading the translation alongside the original text helps them focus better and feel more involved in the prayer.
faq.q7: Can I recite it if I have a busy daily schedule?
faq.a7: Absolutely. Lord Vishnu is the Lord of the Universe and understands the demands of your life. If you have a busy schedule, you can recite it at a time that works for you—whether it is early in the morning before starting your day or at night before sleeping. If you cannot find time to read, you can also listen to an audio version during your commute or while performing household chores. Consistency is always more valuable than the length of the prayer.
